programme.title: Rizzoli & Isles: Post Mortem

programme.title: Rizzoli & Isles: Shadow of Doubt
programme.desc: When a wealthy woman is found dead at the bottom of her stairs, we realize that this was no ordinary slip-and-fall accident. The team is pressed into a game of cat and mouse with a clever killer determined to get away with the perfect crime.

programme.title: Rizzoli & Isles: There Be Ghosts
programme.desc: Jane and Maura must unravel an old ghost story in order to solve a double murder at a hospital. Angela convinces Frankie to stop avoiding Kent and accept his invitation to a concert.

programme.title: Rizzoli & Isles: Dead Weight
programme.desc: When a bicyclist appears to spontaneously combust, Maura proves his bizarre death is actually a murder and helps catch the science-savvy killer. Meanwhile, Jane's invited to teach a class of FBI recruits at Quantico.

programme.title: Rizzoli & Isles: 2M7258-100

programme.title: Rizzoli & Isles: 65 Hours
programme.desc: On the morning that Jane is about to testify in a murder trial, she discovers that a key piece of evidence was stolen. BPD has three days to resolve the crime, or else the murderer may go free. Frankie learns a few magic tricks to impress Nina.

programme.title: Rizzoli & Isles: For Richer or Poorer
programme.desc: When a forensic accountant is gunned down in a park, Jane and Maura are drawn into a sophisticated Ponzi scheme. Jane discovers that her prime suspect is someone from Maura's past. Jane also makes a life-changing career decision.

programme.title: Rizzoli & Isles: Stiffed
programme.desc: A mortician's body is discovered in one of his own funeral home's caskets, leading the team into the world of embalming, interment and ultimately serial murder. Tommy visits with his son TJ and considers returning to Boston for good.

programme.title: Grey's Anatomy: Tell Me Sweet Little Lies
programme.desc: Christina is hesitant to move in with Burke, and George threatens to move out unless Meredith gets rid of their dog. Meanwhile, Alex gets his board-exam results, Meredith takes a blood test for Alzheimer's, and Bailey begins to have contractions.

programme.title: Grey's Anatomy: Break on Through
programme.desc: The nurses at Seattle Grace Hospital go on strike, leaving the doctors to fend for themselves. Meredith's mistake in treating an elderly patient is brought to light by the patient's friends. Meanwhile, Izzy treats a pregnant teen.

programme.title: The Closer: The Other Woman
programme.desc: Brenda is called to testify in a case of Pope vs. Pope, a custody hearing between her boss and his ex wife. Brenda's team investigates a death of a woman whose identity may have been stolen by an addict.

programme.title: The Closer: Borderline
programme.desc: Leaving the scene of an accident to get to a multiple homicide scene, Brenda finds herself unable to drive the streets of L.A. Once she gets to the crime scene, she and the PHD are faced with another challenge.

programme.title: Major Crimes: Jane Doe #38
programme.desc: After finding a young runaway murdered and thrown in a trash can, the Major Crimes unit is led on a long, puzzling hunt to find the deceased girl's killer and her identity. Meanwhile, Ryador consults her husband about a proposition she has for Rusty.

programme.title: Major Crimes: Two Options
programme.desc: The shooting deaths of an elderly couple in their home prompts the team to join forces with the Special Operations Bureau to track down a deadly predator prowling Los Angeles and taking prisoners along the way.

programme.title: Major Crimes: Cutting Loose
programme.desc: Lt. Mike Tao of Major Crimes finds himself doing double duty when the star of the cop show on which he moonlights as a consultant becomes the prime suspect in the murder of his biggest fan.

programme.title: Rizzoli & Isles: Stiffed
programme.desc: A mortician's body is discovered in one of his own funeral home's caskets, leading the team into the world of embalming, interment and ultimately serial murder. Tommy visits with his son TJ and considers returning to Boston for good.

programme.title: How It Really Happened: Siegfried & Roy: Dangerous Illusion (Part 2)
programme.desc: Roy tries to recover from severe wounds inflicted by his beloved tiger Mantacore, but the team’s excuses for the animal’s behavior lose credibility upon further investigation.

programme.title: How It Really Happened: The Monica Lewinsky Scandal
programme.desc: It was the shocking affair that nearly toppled a Presidency; Bill Clinton and a 21 year old White House intern named Monica Lewinsky.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Van-ished
programme.desc: A twelve-year-old girl claimed she had been abducted and sexually assaulted. She recounted what happened in such a flat, unemotional voice that police found it difficult to believe her.

programme.title: Forensic Files: A Novel Idea
programme.desc: When a frantic call from a man claiming his wife fell down the stairs brings investigators to their home, they are concerned by the unusually large number of bloodstains, and how big they are.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Guarded Secrets
programme.desc: A security guard disappears from work without a trace, and investigators can't determine if he left willingly or if foul play was involved.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Smoking Out a Killer
programme.desc: Three college students are killed walking along a footpath over the course of six years. Police know they're dealing with a serial killer, but the hard part is catching him.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Frozen Assets
programme.desc: When a respected business owner is brutally attacked and killed, investigators have to determine if it was a robbery gone wrong or a calculated murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files: House Hunting
programme.desc: When a successful real estate agent is brutally murdered, panicked townspeople begin looking over their shoulders and locking their doors.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Deadly Rebellion
programme.desc: Workers discovered a teen's half-naked body on the side of the road, her throat slit. Police hoped the single foreign hair found in a defensive wound on her thumb would lead them to the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Sign of the Crime
programme.desc: Fire rips through a Seattle woman's apartment and some unsettling evidence is found in the rubble. Shortly after, a second fire breaks out just 100 feet from the first, and evidence points to a serial killer whose skills are evolving.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Palm Saturday
programme.desc: 'The Brian and Beverly Mauck Case - an attractive young couple is found dead in their Graham, Washington home and all signs point to a professional hit. But dogged investigators discover some valuable evidence that proves otherwise.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Runaway Love
programme.desc: A woman is found floating in the harbor of an upscale yacht club, stabbed more than 50 times, and her ex-husband and daughter are missing. Investigators turn to forensic science to determine if they’re searching for victims... or perpetrators.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Fresh Heir
programme.desc: In the 20 years following an unsolved murder, lives changed and so did forensic science. In time, a high-powered microscope and DNA profiling revealed a clue no one had seen before.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Ticker Tape
programme.desc: A victim's autopsy turns an investigation into a landmark forensics case: the device intended to save his life proved to be the only witness to his death.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Step by Step
programme.desc: A wealthy socialite died after falling down stairs, but was it an accident or was it murder? The laws of physics and an accident reconstructionist provided the answer.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Blanket of Evidence
programme.desc: When two women are murdered in a state park, Indiana police suspected a serial killer, until they looked at the evidence more deeply and were brought in totally different directions.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Sworded Scheme
programme.desc: A college student goes missing, and investigators discover blood spatter in her boyfriend’s bedroom. Blood spatter someone has gone to great lengths to conceal.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Unmasked
programme.desc: A serial rapist was on the loose in a Texas town. No one knew where he'd strike next, but the audacity of his attacks and the escalation of violence against his victims made finding him imperative.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Fashion Police
programme.desc: A victim had been stabbed more than thirty times, and the only evidence investigators had was a distinctive button found at the scene.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Church Dis-Service
programme.desc: A young woman attends evening church services... then disappears. Eyewitnesses place her at a nearby gas station, getting into a van. Three days later when her body is discovered, the search for the driver of that van intensifies.

programme.title: Forensic Files: No Safe Place
programme.desc: A wealthy man is gunned down outside his Louisiana home. His wife tells a tale of three masked men emerging from the shadows, but she may know more than she's saying.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Live Wire
programme.desc: A thin piece of wire and some cryptic numbers on a sales receipt held the key to a brutal and senseless murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Van-ished

programme.title: Death Row Stories: Murder in Miami
programme.desc: In 1987 Kris Maharaj, a wealthy British businessman, is sentenced to death for a double homicide in a luxury Miami hotel. The case seems open and shut until an attorney uncovers powerful evidence supporting an alternative explanation of the killings.

programme.title: Death Row Stories: Eye for an Eye
programme.desc: After seventeen years on death row for a mass murder at Chuck E. Cheese's restaurant in Aurora, Nathan Dunlap's fate resides in the hands of one man, Governor John Hickenlooper.

programme.title: Death Row Stories: Double Jeopardy
programme.desc: The wife and two children of an Air Force captain are murdered in Fayetteville, North Carolina, and Army sergeant Tim Hennis is sentenced to death for the crime.

programme.title: Death Row Stories: Memory of Murder
programme.desc: A small-time Ohio drug dealer sits on death row for murdering a family, until a rookie attorney unearths long-hidden case records that shed new light on the case.

programme.title: Death Row Stories: Snitch Work
programme.desc: A 4-year-old girl is found murdered, her body placed in a TV box. Years later, a neighbour is arrested for the crime. When an author seeking to write about the death penalty comes across the case, he finds an investigation filled with holes.

programme.title: Fast N' Loud: '71 Scat Pack Challenger, Part II
programme.desc: Aaron and his team attempt to get the '71 Challenger build to accept the 2015 Challenger technology. In the process, they create a one of a kind resto/ mod. Richard buys a busted up '34 Plymouth and the crew takes the '57 Shorty bus for a test drive.

programme.title: Street Outlaws: Mega Cash Days: Mo Money Mo Problems
programme.desc: Sixty-four of the fastest racers from all over the country arrive to compete in Mega Cash Days, the first double elimination race of its kind. With over $300,000 up for grabs throughout the event, everyone is ready to lay it all on the line for each win.

programme.title: Street Outlaws: Full Throttle: Take Back These Streets
programme.desc: With added features and bonus scenes: The kings of the street return to reclaim their roots. Chief puts the 405 to the test with the biggest list shakeup to date before parting ways with Murder Nova.

programme.title: Street Outlaws: A New Number One
programme.desc: Chief, Dave, Ryan, Monza and Dominator battle for the number one spot as the list shakeup continues. Chief hopes to prove that his split with Shawn hasn't slowed him down. Ryan gets some advice from an unlikely source.

programme.title: Street Outlaws: The Empire Builds Back
programme.desc: Street Outlaws returns and OKC's biggest rivals attack the list with a vengeance. Jackie Knox wants on the list. Chief and Doc square off. Farmtruck and AZN go fishing, trying to give hell to a Hellcat.

programme.title: Street Outlaws: A New Number One
programme.desc: Chief, Dave, Ryan, Monza and Dominator battle for the number one spot as the list shakeup continues. Chief hopes to prove that his split with Shawn hasn't slowed him down. Ryan gets some advice from an unlikely source.

programme.title: Street Outlaws: The Empire Builds Back
programme.desc: Street Outlaws returns and OKC's biggest rivals attack the list with a vengeance. Jackie Knox wants on the list. Chief and Doc square off. Farmtruck and AZN go fishing, trying to give hell to a Hellcat.

programme.title: Dual Survival: Mayan Mayhem
programme.desc: Trapped in a labyrinth of underground tunnels, Joe and Matt facedown darkness and risk drowning only to emerge on surface and find an inhospitable jungle. Their opposing approaches to survival pit them against one another before bringing them to rescue.

programme.title: Dual Survival: On the Edge
programme.desc: Lost on a perilous ridgeline, at 15,000 feet above sea level, Joe and Matt are forced to descend slippery cliffs through extreme weather conditions, where altitude sickness and hypothermia threaten, and life-preserving fire eludes them.

programme.title: Dual Survival: Winter Vortex: Part One
programme.desc: In the snowiest part of Wyoming, Matt and Joe demonstrate how to repurpose a broken down snowmobile, make shelter from snow, and survive sub-freezing temperatures in whiteout conditions that cause them to miss a chance at rescue.

programme.title: Dual Survival: Winter Vortex: Part Two
programme.desc: Stranded in Wyoming's snowy mountains, Matt and Joe are threatened by hypothermia and predatory mountain lions. In frigid conditions, the pair takes their biggest risk yet by splitting up.

programme.title: Dual Survival: Out of the Ashes, Part I
programme.desc: On the slopes of a tropical volcano, Matt and Joe face roadblocks in their quest to navigate towards the coast including thick rainforest, steep-walled gorges and towering waterfalls that stop them dead in their tracks.

programme.title: Dual Survival: Out of the Ashes, Part II
programme.desc: Joe and Matt continue their struggle to cross a volcanic island covered with jungle and water-filled canyons. Every setback fuels a rift between the guys and only a successful hunt can get them out alive.

programme.title: Dual Survival: Waterlogged
programme.desc: Off the beaten path and waterlogged in the rainforest of Costa Rica, Matt and Joe battle torrential rains, deadly insects, and raging rapids. After missing a shot at rescue, they combine survival ingenuity with indigenous skills to get out alive.

programme.title: Moonshiners: Master Distiller: Mark vs. Digger vs. Tim
programme.desc: Tickle hosts as Appalachia's most notorious moonshiners go head-to-head in a moonshine distilling competition. Mark, Digger and Tim run their own secret recipes to be judged by the public to determine once and for all who makes the best shine.

programme.title: Moonshiners: Absinthe Makes the Heart Grow Fonder
programme.desc: Tim and his help struggling owners of Missouri Ridge Distillery in hopes of turning it from problem to profit. Mark and Digger confront a shiner who's been edging into their territory. Lance surprises Jeff with an unorthodox moonshine ingredient.

programme.title: Moonshiners: Master Distiller: High Proof Cherry Bounce
programme.desc: Legal and outlaw distillers compete to make a 300-year-old spirit called Cherry Bounce, a favorite drink of George Washington, nearly lost to time. Josh Owens, whose family kept the recipe alive for generations in the backwoods, joins as guest judge.

programme.title: Moonshiners: Master Distiller
programme.desc: A subculture of distillers across America carries on the backwoods tradition of moonshining. Now, judged by legends Mark, Digger and Tim, America's top legal and outlaw shiners go head to head to see who has what it takes to be called Master Distiller.

programme.title: Moonshiners: Master Distiller: Navy Strength Rum
programme.desc: Outlaw shiners and legal distillers compete to distill true Navy Strength Rum, but the bar for entry is 114-proof. With Tickle as honorary pirate and judged by shiners Mark, Digger and Tim, only one distiller can earn the title of Master Distiller.

programme.title: Dr. Pimple Popper: The Incredible Shrinking Lipoma
programme.desc: Gerald is back to see Dr. Lee and a hand specialist about a huge, intrusive bump on his middle finger. Vinnie has a large, embarrassing birthmark on his neck. Leta has a lump on the back of her thigh.

programme.title: Dr. Pimple Popper: Nose No Bounds
programme.desc: April is covered in bumps that have disfigured her arms. Genner has flown all the way from the Philippines to have Dr. Lee help with his bumpy nose. Chris suffers from psoriasis that nearly covers his body and a bump on his head.

programme.title: Dr. Pimple Popper: Runaway Bump, Never Coming Back?
programme.desc: Leonard has an alarming bump hanging off of his arm that looks like it's breaking through the skin. Brandon has several lumps on his head that makes him fearful for his life. Jess got a noticeable lump on her butt after falling down the stairs.

programme.title: Dr. Pimple Popper: Every Rosacea Has Its Thorn
programme.desc: Patrick's nose is covered by huge bumps and he can barely breathe. Aziza and Lauren are friends who bonded over their matching wrist lumps. Joe has a mound in the middle of his forehead that looks like a third eye.

programme.title: Dr. Pimple Popper: Cystic River
programme.desc: Dennis has several giant growths on the back of his head. Juan has a rare condition he hopes Dr. Lee will understand. Natalie searches for answers as to why her body is covered in hundreds of mysterious dark bumps.

programme.title: Dr. Pimple Popper: Don't Sweat It!
programme.desc: Audrey pops the painful bumps that cover her body so she can feel relief. Felicia has a peculiar looking dark growth on her forehead. Krista's excessive sweating is so severe that on any given day she could fill up an entire milk jug with her sweat.

programme.title: 1000-lb Sisters: Wake-Up Call
programme.desc: Tammy and Amy blame each other for their issues in therapy. When Tammy gets devastating news from Dr. Procter, her brother Chris makes a life-altering decision. A very pregnant Amy can't stop overeating, and the results are disastrous.

programme.title: My Feet Are Killing Me: ET Fingers
programme.desc: Dr. Vincent tries to save a man's enormous toe, as Dr. Schaeffer has his hands full treating a little boy with big demands, and a man with incredibly long toes.

programme.desc: Meri takes Kody to check out the B&B she wants to buy, and Janelle's daughter, Maddie, goes into labor. She's doing a home birth with no epidural. It's a long and painful birth but the Browns are eager to meet their first grandchild.

programme.title: Say Yes to the Dress: It Fits, She Scores!
programme.desc: Kristi is marrying a pro-baseball pitcher and is looking for a glitzy dress to compliment her luxurious lifestyle. Sports fan Jenna wants a dress that will knock it out of the park. Jennifer hopes that her boss will help her find the dress of her dreams.

programme.title: Say Yes to the Dress: Nothing Ordinary Here
programme.desc: Erin wants a blue dress for her destination wedding, but she must first convince her skeptical entourage. Kate is in search of a unique dress that incorporates colour. Heather's entourage is shocked when she proudly shows off her flower-patterned gown.

programme.title: Say Yes to the Dress: More, More, More!
programme.desc: Rasha needs a huge ball gown that will wow all 700 guests at her wedding. Melissa's dad has a special surprise for her when she wants more bling. Burcu needs a personal touch from Pnina when she finds her dress to be lacking in sparkle.

programme.title: Say Yes to the Dress: Drama, Drama, Drama!
programme.desc: WyQuasia's family spends more time bickering with each other than listening to what the bride wants! Shelley's enormous entourage all have so many opinions they make dress shopping impossible. Bride Zabrina's mother demands her custom gown be redesigned.

programme.title: My Big Fat Fabulous Life: Sink or Swim
programme.desc: During a weekend trip to the lake, Whitney and Ryan clash as her friends beg her to ditch him and move back to Greensboro. When Buddy's new girlfriend crashes the party, Whitney worries about losing her 'quarantine husband' to another woman.

programme.title: My Big Fat Fabulous Life: The Buddy System
programme.desc: Wary of being alone in Charlotte, Whitney convinces Buddy to move in with her. With Buddy's new girlfriend taking up his time, however, she finds herself lonelier than ever.

programme.title: Tiny at 20
programme.desc: At just 3-foot-3, Hannah is one of the world's tiniest women. Now in her 20's she's eager to gain her independence and looking to succeed in the world of entertainment.

programme.title: Tallest Teens
programme.desc: Meet three of the tallest teenagers in America and learn how they fit into a society designed for people several feet shorter and how will their size impact their future.

programme.title: Hoarding: Buried Alive: She Wants It, She Takes It
programme.desc: Rhonda's home is filled with dumpster items and she has been forced to live in a camper in her backyard. Now, with neighbors threatening lawsuits and worse, Rhonda must clean up or risk losing everything.

programme.title: Hoarding: Buried Alive: Three Apartments, Three Hoarders
programme.desc: An apartment complex has not one, not two, but three extreme hoarders. For years their hoarding has gone unchecked but with building now under strict management, everything is about to change.

programme.title: My 600-lb Life: Seana's Story
programme.desc: Seana is 23 and nearly 700 lbs, living at her mom's house. She is depressed and anxious and her weight-gain threatens to take her last breath. Dr. Now can give her the tools she needs to live, but until she chooses life, no one can save Seana but Seana.

programme.desc: Joe, Sal, Q and Murr cruise the food court sending texts to strangers, then throw caution, and groceries, to the wind. And tonight's loser is cast as a genie in a play but wishes he could be anywhere else.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Flatfoot the Pirate
programme.desc: Q, Sal, Joe and Murr explain why they've been doused with water at the mall, and tonight's big loser plays a pirate but won't find any rum in his cup.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Remember the Pact
programme.desc: Murr, Sal, Joe and Q beg shoppers to hold on to a weird item, then teach tech to seniors. Tonight's loser reveals his embarrassing digital life to his dad.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Silence of the Lame
programme.desc: It's all play and no work when the guys square off in a toy-pitching competition. And tonight's big loser has to quiet down patrons in a food court.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: The Party Crasher
programme.desc: Joe, Sal, Q and Murr dodge shakedowns with the help of some strangers, and tonight's loser has a run-in at a party that leaves him down for the count.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Dover and Out
programme.desc: The guys survey employees with odd questions, and tonight's loser unveils a mural in a small town whose residents react with big city rage.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Lords of the Ring
programme.desc: The guys skirt the law while posing as receptionists, then go fishing at an unlikely locale. And the heat is on tonight's big losers as they try to give one unlucky customer the deal of a lifetime.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: No Child Left Behind
programme.desc: The guys pitch toys that should be kept away from tots, face off at a market, and see sports at the mall. The big loser sticks his nose where it doesn't belong.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: The Prize Fighter
programme.desc: The guys get their checks bounced by customers who notice unusual charges, then help out coworkers with some peculiar trust exercises. Later, the big loser finds himself deep at a carnival game that's rigged for him to lose.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: The Paternity Test
programme.desc: Joe, Sal, Q and Murr pose as concierges in a hotel lobby, and then commit insurance fraud with the help of some confused strangers. Afterwards, tonight's loser suffers a personal punishment that's anything but child's play.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Rock Botton
programme.desc: The guys 'do the farts' with actor Jeff Daniels, and then try to get strangers to take their sides in ridiculous arguments. Later, tonight's loser finds himself up against a wall around a group of college students.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: It's Electric
programme.desc: The guys pitch whimsical TV show idea to focus groups and then go to the park with their 'daughters' who share unpopular opinions. The loser heads to the spa for an electrifying punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Ruffled Feathers
programme.desc: The guys conduct focus groups to sell their illogical inventions created by the other guys, then head to a shoe store where they get off on the wrong foot. Plus, tonight's big loser discovers he’s more flight than fight at the zoo.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: You're Cut Off
programme.desc: The guys pose as employees at a copy centre, conduct bizarre exit interviews for moviegoers and then lead some very peculiar protests. Later, it's unhappy hour for tonight's big loser, as he cuts off paying customers at a party.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: HellCopter
programme.desc: Joe, Sal, Murr and Q pose as unreceptive receptionists in an office waiting room, then head to a wax museum where they mingle with the stiff crowd. And in tonight’s double punishment, two losers are taken on a chopper ride from hell.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: The Taunted House
programme.desc: The guys get extra cheesy at a pizza parlour, and then head to Wall Street where they ask strangers to help translate bizarre text messages in Spanish. Then, trapped in a haunted house, a nightmare comes to life for tonight's big loser.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Scaredy Cat
programme.desc: Find out which Joker almost walked out of a challenge, and what scene inspired the name of a fan's baby. Plus, learn which Joker is allergic to cats.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Down in the Dump
programme.desc: Find out what objects other than pencils the Jokers have considered putting on people, and whose sister frequently helps on the show. Plus, learn what prop has become a fan favorite.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Sweat the Small Things
programme.desc: Find out what Q considers one of the worst punishments of all time, and what prank Joe often pulls on Sal when they're traveling.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Film Fail
programme.desc: Learn why the Joker film in the produce section of grocery stores, what the guys tease Murr about and why the audio team had issues recording in a medical office.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Moving Dragon
programme.desc: While working at a pool store, Michael flies on a kickboard. Then, at a pet store, he breeds hamsters from pellets. He also stuns his helper on moving day with a fiery statue.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Chilly Flakes and Wormholes
programme.desc: Michael works in a spice shop and gets an icy treatment with his take on 'chilly flakes'. Plus, he cleans a spill at a hotel with his phone. Afterwards, at an abandoned house, he amazes his assistant by climbing through a wormhole.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Cat Lady
programme.desc: At auditions, Michael wows directors with costume changes but cracks when he sings. Plus, things get sticky when he demos flying post-its. Then, Michael's co-worker's jaw drops when a shape-shifting tenant returns while they clean house.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: The Good, the Bad and the Uncomfortable
programme.desc: The guys talk ticks, tricks and antics. Plus, see the Jokers’ best bloopers and learn all about their annoying habits that aren’t captured on camera.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Clash of the Jokers
programme.desc: In this competitive episode, Sal, Joe, Q and Murr battle head-to-head in fierce joker vs. joker challenges. The guys go two-on-two as they conduct focus groups where they focus mostly on making things uncomfortable.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Inside Carbonaro: A Positive Presence
programme.desc: Learn which trick Michael has been performing since he was a child, who embarked on a 21-day juice diet cleanse and what moment had everyone on set erupting with laughter.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Inside Carbonaro: A Living Trilobite
programme.desc: Find out which trick went viral on a hip-hop blog, what word is hard for Michael to pronounce and what degree Michael earned in college.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Inside Carbonaro: It's a Makeshifte
programme.desc: Find out why Michael had to get scorpion training, what animal the crew chased around a store and where Michael's favourite vacation spots are.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Inside Carbonaro: Might Be Trippin'
programme.desc: Find out which trick nearly wounded a delivery man, what illusion is difficult for even skilled magicians to pull off and why fans speculated the show was connected to the illuminati.

programme.title: World's Dumbest...: World's Dumbest Drivers 13
programme.desc: Warning: Driving clips in this show are dumber than they appear! A nitro-burning school bus, recreation vehicles used for purposes other than fun and a prominent baseball player who puts up big numbers on the breathalyser are featured.

programme.title: World's Dumbest...: World's Dumbest Drivers 14
programme.desc: Marvel at the man who drives his car through the front door and down the hallways of city hall. Another drunk driver is too intoxicated to order takeout, and a guy takes an expensive sports car for a test drive...and totals it.

programme.title: World's Dumbest...: World's Dumbest Drivers 16
programme.desc: An over sized man tries to outrun a cop car, while a forklift operator totals an entire warehouse full of alcohol! Plus, dumb drivers crash into the same unlucky bakery.

programme.title: World's Dumbest...: World's Dumbest Drivers 18

programme.title: The Bradshaw Bunch: Three Daughters, Three Problems
programme.desc: Terry’s eldest daughter Rachel’s new real estate career needs help, Lacey’s husband doesn’t want to move to Texas and Erin has been keeping a secret from him about a recent change to her appearance.

programme.title: The Bradshaw Bunch: Terry Is in a Jam
programme.desc: The Bradshaw clan travels to Dallas as Terry is hosting (and driving in) the big Monster Jam truck rally. When Terry suffers from a panic attack, an old sibling rivalry is forced to come to a head.

programme.title: The Bradshaw Bunch: Mr. Wrong
programme.desc: Rachel is with the wrong guy and everyone in the family can see it but her. While Terry and Zurie get payback on Tammy after years of her pranks, Lacey and Noah consider moving back to Texas to be closer to family.

programme.title: The Bradshaw Bunch: A Few Good Men
programme.desc: Terry is invited to Washington D.C. to sing with Rob Gronkowski at the USO. While he brings his family along to meet the troops, Rachel gets her mojo back and falls for a marine.

programme.title: The Bradshaw Bunch: Quarantine Crazy
programme.desc: As the Coronavirus pandemic grips the nation, Terry invites the family to hunker down with him at the ranch, driving everyone crazy.

programme.title: The Bradshaw Bunch: Man Problems
programme.desc: Terry and the girls get involved in Rachel’s love life, resulting in a competition to find her Mr. Right. The family tries to talk Terry out of agreeing to be a potential spokesperson for a certain product.

programme.title: The Bradshaw Bunch: Fit For The Fastlane
programme.desc: Terry prepares to announce at a NASCAR race, which will be his first public appearance since the pandemic started. Wanting to look his best, he tries getting back into shape while trying to convince Rachel to sing at the speedway.

programme.title: The Bradshaw Bunch: Oui Oui Terr-y

programme.title: The Bradshaw Bunch: Top Chef
programme.desc: Terry and Tammy attempt one last time to try and convince Lacey and Noah to move to Texas by offering to buy them a restaurant. Meanwhile, Rachel introduces her new guy to the family by pulling a major prank on Terry.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: The Big Launch

programme.title: 100 Day Dream Home: Grand Space for Grandkids

programme.title: Ty Breaker: Small Home, Big Family Dilemma

programme.title: Rehab Addict: Leaky Bedroom Disaster

programme.title: Rehab Addict: Solarium Refresh
programme.desc: Once a boarded-up eyesore, Nicole's first-floor solarium has the potential to be the best room in the house.

programme.title: Rehab Addict: Jack and Jill Suite

programme.title: Rehab Addict: Grand Garage

programme.title: Rehab Addict: Family Affair

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: Out With the Old, in With the Bold
programme.desc: Tamara modernizes a Tudor home in desperate need of being brought out of the 70's. The outdated kitchen gets a much-needed upgrade while two uninspired living spaces get transformed into statement making rooms on the first floor.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: Cistern Act
programme.desc: Tamara Day buys a 1910 Victorian farmhouse that, with the exception of the kitchen, has been completely gutted. Tamara decides to add a half-bath to the first floor, but a misplaced staircase may pose a problem to her renovations.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: Posthaste in a Shirtwaist
programme.desc: Tamara renovates a 1901 Shirtwaist that was abandoned by a previous renovator who was in over his head. She refurbishes original corbels for the walls and builds a custom range hood for the open plan kitchen.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: Classic Meets Modern Farm

programme.title: Southern Charm: Roamin' Holiday
programme.desc: As Cameran gets a sneak peek at motherhood, Kathryn's trip to Malibu proved to be more than just sun bathing and surfing. Meanwhile, Shep confronts Craig once again about the true nature of his legal career, but leaves their friendship on the field.

programme.desc: Tony Harris and his team investigate the mythical Orang Pendek as well as levitation and a possible developed-immunity to electrocution.

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There: Escape from Monkey Island
programme.desc: Tony Harris and his team examine evidence surrounding mysterious objects spotted over Miami and strange remains that could be from an escaped mutant monkey.

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There: Giant Serpents and Tiny Goblins
programme.desc: Tony Harris's team examines videos of a large serpent, several strange rings and what may be an infamous lake monster.

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There: Alien Orbs and Lightning Balls
programme.desc: Tony Harris's team looks into accounts of strange objects flying over Wisconsin, a strange sound recorded in a North Carolina backyard and several airplanes that seem to defy the laws of physics.

programme.title: The UnXplained: Mysterious Structures
programme.desc: For centuries, archaeologists have tried to figure out how and why structures like the Great Pyramid at Giza, the staircase at Loretto Chapel, and Coral Castle were built.

programme.title: The UnXplained: Evil Places
programme.desc: Deadly forests, haunted houses, and mountains where visitors never make it back alive.

programme.title: The UnXplained: Vampires and Werewolves
programme.desc: Vampires and Werewolves are grotesque, legendary monsters that feed on blood and flesh, and they may be real.

programme.title: The UnXplained: Mysterious Curses
programme.desc: Throughout history, many have believed that places, things and people can be tainted with dark curses. The existence of these mysterious curses is examined.

programme.title: The UnXplained: The Truth About UFOs
programme.desc: Debate over what 'UFOs' really are has raged on for over a decade. The most controversial theory is that these objects are spacecraft from outer space.

programme.title: The UnXplained: Life Beyond Death
programme.desc: We explore the mystery of what happens to us after we die. Clues may lie in unusual cases of near-death experiences, reincarnation, and how memories can be passed between organ donors and recipients.

programme.title: The UnXplained: Mysteries of the Bible
programme.desc: The Bible is inarguably the most important historical text ever written, but its true nature may have been lost or deliberately concealed over time.

programme.title: Movie: Guess Who's Coming to Dinner?
programme.desc: Parents examine their beliefs when their daughter announces her interracial engagement.

programme.title: Movie: A Warm December
programme.desc: A physician helps a woman with sickle-cell anemia while she is being pursued.

programme.title: You Got to Move
programme.desc: Follow those involved in grassroots movements to effect change in America's south.

programme.title: Movie: Say Amen, Somebody
programme.desc: Documenting the American gospel music scene, with special focus on two of the movement's pioneering forces, Willie May Ford Smith and Thomas A. Dorsey.

programme.title: Jazz on a Summer's Day
programme.desc: Legendary documentary of the 1948 Newport Jazz Festival.

programme.title: No Maps on my Taps
programme.desc: Acclaimed documentary on tap dancing.

programme.title: The Blues Accordin' to Lightnin' Hopkins
programme.desc: A portrait of the great Texas bluesman, including interviews and a performance.

programme.title: Movie: Crisis: Behind a Presidential Commitment
programme.desc: Examine how President Kennedy found a diplomatic solution to prevent Governor George Wallace from blocking the enrollment of black students at the University of Alabama.

programme.title: Movie: Broadway Babies
programme.desc: A chorus girl falls for a bootlegger, but changes her mind and marries another.

programme.title: Movie: Show Girl in Hollywood
programme.desc: A show girl gets a break after a producer sees her performing in a nightclub.

programme.title: Movie: Employees' Entrance
programme.desc: An unscrupulous department store manager stops at nothing to get what he wants.

programme.title: Movie: National Velvet
programme.desc: A former jockey helps a young girl train her beloved horse for a difficult steeplechase.

programme.title: Movie: Red Dust
programme.desc: The owner of a rubber plantation in Indochina has an affair with a worker's wife.

programme.title: Movie: The Women
programme.desc: A woman's marital problems fuel the gossip within a circle of Park Avenue women.

programme.title: Jessie: G.I. Jessie
programme.desc: Jessie returns to Texas with the Ross kids in tow, excited to show them the military base where she was raised and introduce them to her father, only to learn the surprising news that her dad is engaged to be married to her high school rival's mother.

programme.title: Movie: Zombies 2
programme.desc: A cheerleader and a football players are preparing to go off to their prom. They don't realize that they are facing danger, in the form of teenage werewolves that threaten to derail their plans for the perfect night.

programme.title: Secrets of Sulphur Springs: Secrets of Sulphur Springs
programme.desc: Griffin Campbell and his family have just uprooted their lives to move to a new town and take ownership of an abandoned hotel in hopes of restoring it to the lively vacation destination it once was.

programme.title: Gabby Duran and the Unsittables: Enter the Dranis
programme.desc: Gabby dismisses Principal Swift's concern and accepts a babysitting gig with a special client on a vacation planet. Unfortunately, it looks like Swift was right to be concerned!

programme.title: Raven's Home: Dream Moms
programme.desc: When Booker and Levi get into some trouble backstage at a concert, Raven and Chelsea try to seize the moment to perform with their all-time favorite group La Dee Da.

programme.title: Raven's Home: Because
programme.desc: Raven opens up about her own visions to the kids, sharing a special moment with Booker. The two team up to try and locate the missing money. Nia feels left out and fakes having a vision.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Elevator Action / Bad Influencer
programme.desc: Gloria gets stuck on an elevator with the Greens. Remy falls under the sway of an internet influencer.

programme.title: Bunk'd: Squatters' Fights
programme.desc: When Ava, Destiny, and Gwen discover that the Grizzly cabin has a much larger bathroom than theirs, they hatch a plan to convince the boys to switch.

programme.title: Bunk'd: Three Stars and a Baby
programme.desc: When Lou asks the campers to fill out counselor evaluations, Ava fears Destiny and Gwen's ratings and worries that her job may be in jeopardy.

programme.title: Sydney to the Max: The Lyin' King
programme.desc: Sydney and Olive's bond is tested when they both become friends with the new girl and keep it behind each other's backs. Young Max and Leo try out for the basketball team.

programme.title: Jessie: To Be Me or Not to Be Me
programme.desc: Jessie gets her first big acting part in a Shakespeare production in the park. When Jessie and Zuri switch personalities, that leaves an eight-year-old trying to pull off Jessie's performance and keep from ruining it for her.

programme.title: Jessie: Toy Con
programme.desc: Zuri must find a way to get back a cherished toy that Jessie passed on to her after she trades it to a toy collector. Meanwhile, Luke and Emma work together to teach Bertram Salsa dancing so he can compete in a dance contest to win his crush's heart.

programme.title: Raven's Home: Sorry to Father You
programme.desc: When Raven and Chelsea go on a weekend getaway to celebrate the anniversary of their friendship, the kids are left at home in the care of both Devon and Garrett.

programme.title: Raven's Home: The Foreign Identity
programme.desc: When Nia develops a crush on the new French exchange student, Timothée, she pretends to be a sophisticated Brit to win him over and enlists Booker to help her keep up the ruse.

programme.title: Liv and Maddie: Steal-A-Rooney
programme.desc: When Maddie tries to set Liv up with a new friend, Liv gets offended and explains she can find her own friends. Unfortunately, this drives Liv towards bad girl Kylie Kramer.

programme.title: Liv and Maddie: Brain-A-Rooney
programme.desc: Liv convinces Joey to let her join his Brain Olympics team, even though he doesn’t think she’s smart enough – and she’s set on proving him wrong.

programme.title: Austin & Ally: Family and Feuds
programme.desc: Austin, Ally and Trish join Dez at a birthday celebration for Dez's younger sister, Didi. To everyone's surprise, Didi introduces her new boyfriend who is none other than Dez's nemesis, Chuck!

programme.title: Austin & Ally: Real Life and Reel Life
programme.desc: When Dez and Trish decide to make a movie about Austin and Ally's partnership, the gang re-enacts past moments and events that retell their story.

programme.title: Jessie: Moby and Scoby
programme.desc: When Jessie goes back to college, her and Ravi end up sharing the same class. Zuri brings home a baby giraffe from Africa.

programme.title: Jessie: Basket Case
programme.desc: Eager to play in Luke's basketball game, Ravi unexpectedly runs into LA Clippers star Chris Paul, who he enlists to coach him. Meanwhile, Jessie learns that Hudson is her new classmate and is unhappily forced to partner with him for an assignment.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Friend Con/ Flimflammed
programme.desc: Chip Whistler and Bill hit it off and become friends at Farm Con. Cricket stumbles into having one hundred dollars and immediately buys into a pyramid scheme.

programme.title: Miraculous: Tales of Ladybug and Cat Noir: Party Crasher
programme.desc: Because of a misunderstanding, a fan of Adrien's gets akumatized into Party Crasher. Convinced that his star has made a fool of him, he is determined to get revenge.

programme.title: Sofia the First: Mystic Meadows
programme.desc: Sofia’s Buttercup troop visits Mystic Meadows, a fantastic retirement home for sorcerers. Sofia decides that her 'good deed for the day' will be to help Cedric earn the respect of his father, Goodwyn the Great.

programme.title: Sofia the First: Princesses to the Rescue!
programme.desc: On a royal visit to the Kingdom of Wei-Ling, James and Prince Jin run off to find the treasure of the Jade Tiger. King Roland and Emperor Quon go to rescue the boys but find themselves trapped. Features princess Mulan.

programme.title: Bluey: Hammerbarn / Feather Wand / Dance Mode
programme.desc: While on a trip to the store, Bluey and Bingo create their own mini-homes side-by-side in the trolley. Later, when Bingo isn't invited to Chloe's party, she uses a magic feather wand to make things incredibly heavy.

programme.title: Bluey: Army / Fancy Restaurant / Stumpfest

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Scuba-Doggies/ Walking the Bob
programme.desc: Bingo and Rolly go on an underwater adventure to retrieve a lost package. After a visit to the eye doctor, it's up to the pups to lead a blurry-eyed Bob home.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: New Year, New Bob / All for Show
programme.desc: Two puppies who love to have fun take on adventures around their neighbourhood.

programme.title: T.O.T.S.: Temple of the Tiger/ The Gift-Mazing Birthday
programme.desc: Freddy and Pip try their hardest to keep a baby tiger prince clean; Freddy tries to find the perfect birthday present for one of the new babies.

programme.title: T.O.T.S.: Panda Excess/ A Stinky Situation
programme.desc: Pip and Freddy must deliver a baby panda so cute that it makes it hard to say goodbye. Later, the fliers have trouble delivering a baby skunk due to its overwhelming smell.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: A New Pup in Town / The Last Pup-icorn
programme.desc: When Chloe's new puppy, Keia, runs away, the pups must find her before Chloe returns! When Chloe wishes she could have a unicorn, Bingo, Rolly and Keia set out to find one.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Valentine Surprise/ Bright Lights, Pup City
programme.desc: The pups look for Valentine's Day gifts for Bob and Chloe; Bob is sad when the Symphony of Lights is canceled, so the pups try to fix the lights.

programme.title: Bluey: Backpackers / The Adventure / Copycat
programme.desc: Mum and Dad go on an adventure with the kids; Bluey and Chloe have an imaginary adventure together; Bluey decides to copy Dad.

programme.title: Bluey: The Show / Ticklecrabs / Escape
programme.desc: The adventures of Bluey, a loveable puppy who lives with her parents and little sister.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Shorts: Al Rojo Vivo
programme.desc: Mickey Mouse finds himself in silly situations around the world.

programme.title: Miraculous: Tales of Ladybug and Cat Noir: Startrain
programme.desc: Max's mom gets akumatized into Startrain. Now at the controls of a flying train, she wants to realize her dream of becoming an astronaut.

programme.title: Miraculous: Tales of Ladybug and Cat Noir: Kwamibuster
programme.desc: Mrs. Mendeleiev gets akumatized into Kwamibuster. She wants to capture Tikki and Plagg to prove to the world that these magic creatures are real. Ladybug and Cat Noir will have to protect their secret at all costs.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Space Chicken/ Steak Night
programme.desc: To impress his new neighbors, Cricket attempts to launch a chicken into space. The Greens are excited to continue their traditional monthly steak dinner, but Cricket accidentally loses the steaks on the subway.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Cricket Versus/ Blue Tater
programme.desc: Cricket must wrestle a wild animal in order to prove himself worthy of the Green family name. When Cricket persuades Bill to convert his pickup into a French fry food truck, he discovers a mysterious blue potato that Gramma says is cursed with bad luck.

programme.title: Raven's Home: Just Call Me Vic
programme.desc: Shorthanded without Chelsea, Grandpa Baxter shows up to help with the chores to make Raven's life easier, but only ends up making everyone's lives more difficult.

programme.title: Raven's Home: New Dog, Old Trick
programme.desc: When Raven accompanies Nia on her retreat for the Young Leaders of Tomorrow, Raven takes advantage of the retreat to try and further her fashion career.

programme.title: Bunk'd: Between A Raccoon and a Hard Place
programme.desc: Gwen befriends a group of campers called the "Extreme Team," known for their gutsy outdoor activities. They invite Gwen to hike to Dead Horse Drop, and she accepts.

programme.title: Bunk'd: MoSquito Mo Problems
programme.desc: Mosquitoes are rampant, so Matteo looks to science to create a repellent. However, he ends up taking credit for something that Gwen invented.

programme.title: U.S. House of Representatives
programme.desc: Floor proceedings of the U.S. House of Representatives.

programme.title: Politics & Public Policy Today
programme.desc: The day's major public affairs and political events presented without interruption.

programme.title: Politics & Public Policy Today
programme.desc: The day's major public affairs and political events presented without interruption.

programme.title: Public Affairs Events
programme.desc: Public affairs events, congressional hearings, speeches, and interviews.

programme.title: Washington Journal
programme.desc: A political call-in program that touches on political and legislative issues.

programme.title: U.S. House of Representatives
programme.desc: Floor proceedings of the U.S. House of Representatives.

programme.title: Florida Man Murders: Killer Succession/ Dial 911 for Murder
programme.desc: A car bomb explodes rocking the millionaire enclave of Naples, Florida. Tobacco heiress Margaret Benson and her son are victims of a double homicide. Her severely injured daughter clings to life, and may be the police’s only hope for an eyewitness.

programme.title: Exhumed: Cold Case, Unearthed
programme.desc: Marilyn McIntyre's gruesome murder goes unsolved for decades, until a phone call resurrects the investigation. Is it possible evidence is buried with the victim? After thirty years underground, does it still exist?

programme.title: Exhumed: Killer Obsession
programme.desc: Murder cases in which unearthing a victim's body is the key to solving the twisted crimes.

programme.title: Framed by the Killer: The Sex Cult Frame
programme.desc: A successful entrepreneur is found dead on the side of the road. Surprise clues are found during the autopsy, and investigators soon uncover a devious sex cult and a masterminded revenge plan that leads them to the one killer no one ever suspected.

programme.title: Catching a Serial Killer: Sam Little
programme.desc: Investigative journalist Beth Karas follows Sam Little, America's most prolific serial killer who confessed to murdering over 90 women in 17 states, in hopes to identify how he got away with murder for so long.

programme.title: An Unexpected Killer: Twisted Betrayal
programme.desc: When a devoted mother with a bright future in show business is ruthlessly gunned down, a number of suspects emerge, but just as detectives think they have the killer in their sights, the investigation takes a jaw dropping turn.

programme.title: One Deadly Mistake: Out of the Darkness
programme.desc: The body of Kathy Blair, a choir director, is found stabbed to death in her bedroom in Austin, Texas. The gruesome crime scene puzzles investigators until an unlikely clue captured in the darkness puts them on the trail of a depraved killer.

programme.title: Snapped: Carol Carlson
programme.desc: When a Kapowsin, Washington mother is discovered murdered on her couch in a compromised position, investigators must piece together all the evidence to determine if this was a random crime of opportunity or a façade to humiliate the victim.

programme.title: Snapped: Janeene Jones
programme.desc: Allegations of real estate fraud and and rumors of murder have Sarasota Police on the tail of a criminal whose final plan to evade trouble lands her in the very hands she tried to avoid.

programme.title: Snapped: Virginia Hyatt
programme.desc: When a Texarkana square dancer is gunned down on the steps of her palatial home, detectives follow a murky trail of surveillance footage leading straight to a killer with a taste for revenge.

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent: Betrayed
programme.desc: A cheating couple run off together and disappear, leaving the wife to pick up the pieces.

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent: Assassin
programme.desc: When the leader of a war-torn nation's Democracy Movement is exiled to the United States, assassins immediately target her. Logan and Wheeler race to identify and capture the hitmen before they succeed in silencing the woman.

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent: Please Note, We Are No Longer Accepting Letters of Recommendation From Kissinger
programme.desc: Three people are shot and killed at close range, but their two-year-olds were left unharmed. It turns out they knew their killer, who was obsessed with entry into an exclusive pre-school.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: A War Zone
programme.desc: Intelligence is tasked with tracking down the dealers of fentanyl, a drug that has been linked to multiple overdoses around the city. As they investigate possible sources, the team uncovers a series of dealers in a college smuggling ring.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: Some Friend
programme.desc: After receiving a complaint, Burgess and Sorensen find the body of a young girl inside a baseball player’s home. There is conflict in the case when it is determined the player is friends with Olinsky.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: 300,000 Likes
programme.desc: Voight calls the team to investigate a homicide involving the only witness in a rape and murder case against an extremely young man. Intelligence investigates the accused's family to see if someone was paid to take out the victim.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: A Shot Heard Round the World
programme.desc: After two officers are murdered by a highly trained and knowledgeable killer, Voight struggles to keep the case contained. At the same time, Lindsay confronts Antonio about his new job.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: Don't Bury This Case
programme.desc: Severide is brought in for questioning after his car is found to have caused a deadly accident. As the investigation gets underway, the team uncovers a group of car-jackers may be the key to Severide's innocence.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: Don't Read the News
programme.desc: After the body of a young woman is found with multiple gunshot wounds, Intelligence discovers this case may be connected with several other cases, and they uncover a serial killer is working in Chicago.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: You Wish
programme.desc: When a mutilated sex offender is found dead, it is unclear if he was the victim of a crime. As intelligence questions those related to the case, they find themselves up against a special police force that may be covering up illegal activities.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: Sanctuary
programme.desc: After a female jogger is brutally murdered in the park, Rixton and Atwater arrive to find two suspects fleeing the scene. When the men enter a church, the priest blocks the police from entering the building.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: I Remember Her Now
programme.desc: The body of a young girl is found crushed in an abandoned building after she runs away from a treatment centre for troubled girls. Voight sends Halstead to work undercover inside the centre, where he believes something shady is happening.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: Seven Indictments
programme.desc: When a charred body is found in a torched house, Intelligence must work to identify the victim, as well as a young boy who is found badly injured inside. As they work towards identifying the victim, they uncover a web of secrets and lies.

programme.title: Black-ish: Good in the 'Hood
programme.desc: Dre's sister Rhonda takes Jack and Diane to her part of town in a black neighbourhood and claims the twins are scared of their own community. Dre and Rhonda take them to a local diner to show them why it's so special.

programme.title: Black-ish: When I Grow Up (To Be a Man)
programme.desc: Jack gets cut from the basketball team and it leads to a family discussion about him being short for his age. Dre is worried for his future, but Bow feels strongly that Jack will overcome his adversity and be stronger for it.

programme.title: Black-ish: Mad and Boujee
programme.desc: When Dre is invited to join a private social club for the black upper class, Bow and the family jump at the opportunity to mingle with this new community, but Dre’s not so sure these exclusive clubs are all they’re cracked up to be.

programme.title: Black-ish: Martin Luther Skiing Day
programme.desc: On the way to the Johnson family's annual Martin Luther King holiday ski trip, Dre realizes his children may not know the significance of Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. so he decides to inundate them with Dr. King history facts.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Ice Cream of Margie (With the Light Blue Hair)
programme.desc: After Homer gets fired at the nuclear power plant, he takes over the ice cream truck business, while a depressed Marge creates popsicle stick sculptures to keep busy. The sculptures quickly become popular and Marge is excited to have a purpose in life.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Haw-Hawed Couple
programme.desc: After Bart convinces all his friends not to go to Nelson's birthday party, Marge forces him to attend. After the party, Bart becomes Nelson's new best friend, and under Nelson's protection, no one dares to mess with Bart.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Kill Gil, Volumes 1 & 2
programme.desc: The family is stuck with an unwanted houseguest after they open their home to Gil for the Christmas holidays. After a full year, an attempt at getting him a job and many failed 'accidents,' Marge finally grows a spine and throws Gil out Christmas Day.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Little Big Girl
programme.desc: Lisa decides to take creative licence in crafting her heritage as a Native American when she discovers that the Simpson family tree is less than exciting. The innocent embellishment leads to her speaking at the National Native American Tribal Council.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Springfield Up
programme.desc: A documentary filmmaker offers an inside look at his work chronicling the growth and development of some of Springfield's finest. Marge puts her photojournalism dream on hold in order to support Homer as he tries out odd jobs in search of success.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Rome-Old and Julie-Eh
programme.desc: When Homer incorrectly files for bankruptcy, the court appoints an accountant for him. Grampa and Selma fall in love and move in together, and Homer and Patty plot to break up their love connection.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Homerazzi
programme.desc: Marge is inspired to purchase a fireproof safe to protect the family's most precious valuables after a candle on Homer's birthday cake sets fire to his party hat. A freak accident causes the safe to explode, forcing Marge to restage the family's photos.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Marge Gamer
programme.desc: Marge takes a chance using the Internet after she becomes a spectacle during a PTA meeting for not having an email address. Marge becomes amazed by what the Internet has to offer and joins a role-playing fantasy game called "Earthland Realms."

programme.title: The Simpsons: Crook and Ladder
programme.desc: Maggie goes on a path of destruction after Marge throws away her pacifier. Marge gives in and sends Homer to the store to get a new pacifier, but he can't find the right brand, so Maggie comes up with her own solution.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Stop, or My Dog Will Shoot!
programme.desc: Santa's Little Helper becomes a local hero and the family enrol him into Police Academy.

programme.title: The Simpsons: 24 Minutes
programme.desc: With the help of Jack Bauer and Chloe O'Brian, Bart and Lisa must stop a group of bullies from releasing the ultimate stink bomb at the school's annual bake sale.

programme.title: The Simpsons: You Kent Always Say What You Want
programme.desc: Kent Brockman uses a shocking expletive during the nightly news broadcast and is fired when the FCC is tipped off. The dejected anchor takes refuge with the Simpsons and Lisa plots a way to get him his position back.

programme.title: It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia: Mac Day
programme.desc: The gang gets a day to do what they want while everyone else must participate willingly. This is Mac's Day and it's got twice the 'Mac'.

programme.title: It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia: The Gang Saves the Day
programme.desc: When a routine trip to a convenience store places the gang in the middle of an armed robbery, each of them is forced to explore how they react in extreme situations.

programme.title: It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia: The Gang Gets Quarantined
programme.desc: With an upcoming singing competition, the gang quarantines themselves inside Paddy's in efforts to save their singing voices in the midst of a flu outbreak.

programme.title: It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia: Flowers for Charlie
programme.desc: When an experiment successfully multiplies Charlie's intellect and takes him away from the bar, the rest of the gang find themselves struggling to complete the menial 'Charlie Work'.

programme.title: Mom: Hot Soup and Shingles
programme.desc: When Christy gets injured, she refuses to admit she needs Bonnie's help. Meanwhile, Luke turns to Baxter for advice about being a father.

programme.title: Mom: Leather Cribs and a Medieval Rack
programme.desc: Christy and Bonnie come clean about secrets they've been keeping from each other. Meanwhile, Violet and Luke discover they have different ideas about parenting.

programme.title: Mom: Fireballs and Bullet Holes
programme.desc: Christy tries to start a relationship with her father, while Bonnie struggles to accept that Christy wants him in her life.

programme.title: Mom: Nietzsche and a Beer Run
programme.desc: Christy is tempted by her old vices when she dates an attractive, hard-partying man. Meanwhile, Bonnie decides her advice is worth money and becomes a life coach.

programme.title: Mom: Jail Jail and Japanese Porn
programme.desc: When Christy puts her new relationship ahead of friends and family, Bonnie has to pick up the slack.

programme.title: Black-ish: Any Given Saturday
programme.desc: Jack and Diane are doing biography projects about each other in school, and Diane decides to shoot a documentary on Jack's budding basketball career.

programme.title: Black-ish: Black Nanny
programme.desc: Dre and Bow start to slip on the kids' activities and chores around the house, so Bow persuades Dre to hire a nanny. After several interviews that don't go well, they meet Vivian, who impresses them with her desire to take care of the whole family.

programme.title: Movie: Downsizing
programme.desc: After a company develops a way to shrink people to a fraction of their normal size as a means of combating overpopulation, a husband and wife decide to go through with the procedure, but discover that the little life isn't everything that was promised.

programme.title: Movie: Mamma Mia! Here We Go Again
programme.desc: A pregnant Sophie grows closer to her mother as she shares stories of how she came to live on a Greek island and how she met three handsome young men, any of whom could be Sophie's father, but an unexpected visit from Sophie's grandmother changes things.

programme.title: The Blue Planet: Open Ocean
programme.desc: Examines the predatory skills of some of the most charismatic hunters found on the planet: whales, dolphins, tuna, shark and rapier-nosed billfish.

programme.title: Planet Earth II: Cities
programme.desc: Rooted on the slope of a rocky hill in Namaqualand, South Africa stands The Namaqua Rock Fig Tree. with the ability to withstand the unforgiving conditions of the surrounding desolate landscape, and with minimal rainfall, it is a testament to survival.

programme.title: Planet Earth II: Islands
programme.desc: For some, remote islands offer sanctuary away from the mainland: the tiny pygmy three-toed sloth only survives because of the peace and safety offered by its Caribbean island home, while seabirds like albatross thrive in predator-free isolation.

programme.title: Planet Earth II: Mountains
programme.desc: In this episode we climb high into the world's great mountain ranges. Only a few pioneering animals have what it takes to survive up here, they are amongst the most elusive and mysterious on the planet.

programme.title: Planet Earth II: Jungles
programme.desc: Jungles are the richest places on Earth. A magical world of surprise, drama and unforgettable wild characters. From the jungles of Brazil to Costa Rica, watch as the animals face life in the most competitive place on Earth.

programme.title: Planet Earth II: Deserts
programme.desc: In this episode we visit the world's deserts, a land of extremes that pushes life to the limit. Animals here have developed ingenious ways of dealing with the hostile conditions giving rise to the most incredible survival stories on Earth.

programme.title: Planet Earth II: Grasslands
programme.desc: Grasslands cover one quarter of all land and support the greatest gatherings of wildlife on Earth. The extraordinary creatures that live here must survive the most hostile seasons on the planet.

programme.title: Planet Earth II: Islands
programme.desc: For some, remote islands offer sanctuary away from the mainland: the tiny pygmy three-toed sloth only survives because of the peace and safety offered by its Caribbean island home, while seabirds like albatross thrive in predator-free isolation.

programme.title: Planet Earth II: Mountains
programme.desc: In this episode we climb high into the world's great mountain ranges. Only a few pioneering animals have what it takes to survive up here, they are amongst the most elusive and mysterious on the planet.

programme.title: Planet Earth II: Jungles
programme.desc: Jungles are the richest places on Earth. A magical world of surprise, drama and unforgettable wild characters. From the jungles of Brazil to Costa Rica, watch as the animals face life in the most competitive place on Earth.

programme.title: Planet Earth II: Deserts
programme.desc: In this episode we visit the world's deserts, a land of extremes that pushes life to the limit. Animals here have developed ingenious ways of dealing with the hostile conditions giving rise to the most incredible survival stories on Earth.

programme.title: Planet Earth II: Grasslands
programme.desc: Grasslands cover one quarter of all land and support the greatest gatherings of wildlife on Earth. The extraordinary creatures that live here must survive the most hostile seasons on the planet.

programme.title: Planet Earth II: Cities
programme.desc: Rooted on the slope of a rocky hill in Namaqualand, South Africa stands The Namaqua Rock Fig Tree. with the ability to withstand the unforgiving conditions of the surrounding desolate landscape, and with minimal rainfall, it is a testament to survival.

programme.title: Attenborough and the Giant Elephant
programme.desc: The amazing life story of the most famous elephant to have ever lived: Jumbo. With scientists from around the world, Sir David has unique access to the elephant's skeleton at the American Museum of Natural History.

programme.title: Planet Earth: The Best of The Hunt
programme.desc: A detailed look at the remarkable strategies the planet's top hunters use to succeed, and the huge effort they must put forth in order to survive.

programme.title: Planet Earth: Enchanted Kingdom
programme.desc: Narrated by Idris Elba, explore seven distinct worlds in the wildest continent on Earth, Africa, to discover that nature is so much more surprising and extraordinary than ever imagined.

programme.title: Planet Earth: Dynasties: Lions
programme.desc: The lioness leader of a once mighty pride must now battle for their very survival.

programme.title: Planet Earth: Dynasties: Chimpanzee

programme.title: Planet Earth: Dynasties: Tiger
programme.desc: A tigress fends off rivals set on taking over her turf, all while protecting her young cubs.

programme.title: Planet Earth: Dynasties: Painted Wolf

programme.title: Planet Earth: Dynasties: Emperor Penguin
